# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.143.0/http/server.ts";
import { kv } from "https://deno.land/std@0.143.0/kv/mod.ts";

async function searchHandler(req: Request) {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const userId = url.searchParams.get("userId");
  const filterParam = url.searchParams.get("filterParam");

  if (!userId || !filterParam) {
    return new Response("Missing userId or filterParam", { status: 400 });
  }

  const client = await kv.open();
  try {
    const key: [string, string, string] = ["users", userId, filterParam];
    const result = await client.get(key);
    
    if (result.value === null) {
      return new Response("No results found", { status: 404 });
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(result.value), {
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response("Internal server error", { status: 500 });
  }
}

serve(searchHandler);
